title: Clamp a pagination window so the last page does not overrun
task: `npm test` fails: `pageBounds` in src/window.js computes the `[start, end)` window for a 1-based page but does not clamp the indices to `total`, so the final partial page (and pages past the end) report a window that overruns the data. Fix it to clamp both indices to `total`, keeping 1-based paging and an empty `[total, total]` window past the end. Do not modify the tests. Run the tests to verify.
notes: An off-by-one/window-overrun distinct from off-by-one-fix (which is about page COUNT rounding): here the boundary bug is a missing clamp on the slice bounds. file_not_contains removes the unclamped end line; the test pin keeps the clamped-window assertion.
